project_goal: Full-stack physics experiment platform: Isaac Sim backend + React web frontend with WebRTC streaming, real-time telemetry, and interactive control.
current_focus: All 8 experiments implemented (web interactive); 1/2/3/7 also have batch CLI
VR hand tracking integration — Meta Quest 3S → UDP → WebRTC server → experiment interaction
Architecture is clean (no dead root scripts, no duplicated PDFs, no orphaned packages)
Preserve project continuity across agent sessions

expt1_angular_momentum.notes: Uses teammate USD model + DynamicCuboid proxies

expt2_large_pendulum.notes: Procedural RK4 VisualPendulum (no PhysX joints). Classmate code integrated: full θ/ω/α telemetry, period detection via zero-crossing, series expansion comparison, batch mode with multi-phase sweep + Markdown report, and web export of a PHY1002-style PDF report populated from Python-generated plots/data.

expt3_ballistic_pendulum.notes: Full web interactive + batch mode. Procedural PhysX scene: kinematic pivot + compound pendulum body (rod + 4-walled cup) via Y-axis revolute joint + ball DynamicCuboid. Zero-restitution high-friction materials make the collision fully inelastic; compound cup geometry traps the ball on impact. Live telemetry streams θ, ω, ball speed, and derives v0_measured from θmax via v0 = (m_ball+m_pend)/m_ball · √(2gL(1−cos θmax)). Batch sim.py runs a v0 sweep and generates swing curves + formula verification plot + Markdown report.

expt4_driven_damped.notes: Driven damped torsional oscillator implemented procedurally in PhysX. Kinematic pivot + dynamic aluminium disk (MassAPI with explicit I_z = ½MR²) joined by a Z-axis RevoluteJoint with UsdPhysics.DriveAPI: stiffness=κ (torsional spring, N·m/rad) and damping=b=γ·I (magnetic brake). Sinusoidal targetPosition = A·sin(2πf·t) updated at 120 Hz → PhysX integrates I·θ̈ + b·θ̇ + κ·θ = κ·A·sin(ωt). Controls: drive frequency f, damping γ, driver amplitude A, spring κ. Buttons: Start Driver / Free Oscillation (initial ω kick with A=0) / Reset / Generate Lab Report. Telemetry: live θ, ω, driver angle θ_d, peak |θ|, and analytical reference values (f₀, f_d, Q = √(κI)/b, theory amplitude, phase lag). Lab-report pipeline (added 2026-04-27): core/exp4_report.py — RK4 ringdown + damped-sine LM fit, RK4 resonance sweeps for 3 damping levels, half-power FWHM extraction, linear-LS sinusoid fit for phase lag (within 0.1° of theory), 4 matplotlib figures, half-amplitude asymmetry index, formal Markdown report following the guideline.pdf rubric (Objective / Method / Raw Data / Data and Error Analysis / Conclusion), full instrumental error propagation (electronic balance, vernier caliper, manufacturer kappa, propagated I and f₀), and answering all six Expt_4.pdf questions. ZIP packaging of plots/CSVs/JSON/Markdown. Frontend Download PDF Report (added 2026-04-27): frontend/src/components/Exp4ReportPDF.tsx renders a 15-page CUHK-Shenzhen formal lab report with @react-pdf/renderer (Times-Roman fonts, codecogs LaTeX equations, academic top/mid/bottom-rule tables, cover page, fixed running header, page numbers) — same toolchain and visual language as Exp 1's Lab_Report_Angular_Momentum.pdf and Exp 2's Lab_Report_Large_Amplitude_Pendulum.pdf. The matplotlib PDF is retained inside the ZIP as an offline-readable backup.

expt5_rotational_inertia.notes: Physical pendulum integrated from classmate code (实验5/expt5_pendulum_sim.py). Procedural PhysX scene: uniform bar on kinematic pivot via Y-axis revolute joint so gravity (-Z) produces real torque. Live telemetry: θ, ω, measured T, theoretical T = 2π√((L²/12 + x²)/(g·x)). Adjustable m, L, x, θ₀. Web report export (2026-04-27 redesign): backend records the run's time-series samples, Python/Matplotlib generates 4 PNG plots + CSV/Markdown/ZIP artifacts; frontend Exp5ReportPDF.tsx composes the formal PHY1002 lab-report PDF with @react-pdf/renderer (matching Exp1 typography: cover page, sections, LaTeX equations via codecogs, horizontal-line tables, headers, page numbers). The Python-side PdfPages PDF has been removed.

expt6_centripetal_force.notes: Centripetal force via PhysX prismatic-spring model (replaces stub). Scene is procedural: kinematic rotor Xform spun about +Z by an async pose-driver at user-set ω (with a ~1.2 s linear ramp to avoid drive jitter); dynamic bob connected to the rotor via a USD PrismaticJoint along rotor-local X with a linear DriveAPI (stiffness=k, damping=c, target=r_target). PhysX integrates the bob motion — the spring stretches until F_spring = k·(r_actual − r_target) balances the centripetal requirement. Measured centripetal force is read directly from the spring extension, and cross-checked by m·v²/r derived from the bob's live PhysX state (no closed-form F=mv²/r fallback). Gravity ON; a frictionless table supports the bob; bob has Z-translation + X/Y rotation locked. Controls (set_exp6_mass, set_exp6_radius, set_exp6_omega, set_exp6_spring_k, set_exp6_damper) + Start/Stop/Reset + Export Lab Report (PDF). Telemetry includes centripetal_force, force_theory, force_kinematic, radius_actual, speed, omega, spring_extension, omega_critical = √(k/m), KE. Report export command export_exp6_report uses recorded PhysX telemetry, Python/Matplotlib plots, Markdown template report_templates/expt6_centripetal_force.md.j2, and returns plot data URLs. The primary user-facing PDF is now rendered in frontend/src/components/Exp6ReportPDF.tsx via @react-pdf/renderer using the formal Experiment 1-style layout; backend PdfPages PDF remains as fallback.

expt7_momentum.notes: Full web interactive: procedural scene, 4-trial state machine, per-cart telemetry, collision detection, PDF report. Integrated from classmate code.

expt8_resonance.notes: 1-D wave equation (∂²u/∂t² = c² ∂²u/∂x² − 2γ ∂u/∂t) integrated by leapfrog FDM in core/exp8_analysis.py. N kinematic 'air-slice' rigid bodies along the tube visualise the wave. Closed/open ends switchable; resonance at L+0.3d=(2n−1)λ/4 (closed) or L+0.6d=nλ/2 (open). Speaker frequency, amplitude, length, damping all sliderable. Real-world frequency is shown to the user; under the hood we scale by EXP8_C_SIM/EXP8_C_REAL so the solver is stable at 240 Hz physics.

public_share.purpose: Expose the frontend to anyone on any network (other lab, campus LAN, off-campus) via an outbound tunnel. No client install or port forwarding on the viewer side.
public_share.blocked_providers_note: Cloudflare Quick Tunnel (trycloudflare.com) and ngrok are unreachable from this cluster; their IP ranges are blocked by egress. share.sh avoids both.
public_share.rate_limit_note: Curling the public URL from INSIDE this container hits a conntrack/NAT limit after ~2 requests (traffic traverses egress twice). External viewers on the campus network or internet do NOT hit this — they only transit the egress once inbound. Always verify by asking a remote user to load the URL, not by curling from this shell.
public_share.video_fallback: Frontend auto-falls-back from WebRTC (UDP, blocked by TCP tunnel) to WS-JPEG (/video_feed) after ~8 s. Remote viewers see a brief spinner, then live frames.

how_to_add_experiment: 1. Build USD physics in Experiment/expN/ (rigid bodies, joints, materials)
2. In core/webrtc_server.py: add state vars, _handle_ws_message cases, telemetry in _telemetry_loop
3. In core/webrtc_server.py: update _switch_camera presets if needed
4. In core/webrtc_server.py: add _read_* helpers for telemetry readback
5. In frontend/src/experiments.ts: set isLocked: false, adjust controls/chartConfig
6. (Optional) Add experiments/exptN_name/ for batch analysis with ExperimentBase
operating_rules: Do not depend on prior chat as the only project memory
Record architecture or workflow decisions as ADRs
Write a handoff note when continuity would otherwise be lost
Update this file when project direction or active priorities change
Keep rule files and AGENTS.md versioned in git

layers_hardened: WebSocket telemetry (heartbeat=15-20s, parallel per-client send with 0.5s timeout, dead-client sweeper, throttled error logging)
WebRTC video (multi-STUN, 250ms capture timeout with last-good-frame cache, 2s Replicator init cooldown, two-stage ICE recovery)
WS-JPEG fallback (heartbeat, fast retry on close)
Frontend WS service (exp-backoff reconnect, 15s heartbeat, 30s stale watchdog, replay enter_experiment, visibilitychange fast retry)
WebRTC viewer (restartIce + full reconnect, 6s stall watchdog, WS-JPEG → WebRTC upgrade probe)

open_risks: exp8 USD prim paths in webrtc_server.py are guesses — must match actual USD structure
Experiment runtime behavior needs live Isaac Sim verification

exp6 kinematic rotor drives the prismatic joint via pose updates — if PhysX fails to pick up the changing constraint velocity at very high ω (> ~10 rad/s), increase EXP6_SOLVER_POS_ITERS further or switch the rotor to a dynamic body driven by a velocity target on a Z-axis revolute joint to the world anchor
VR hand tracking requires same-LAN Quest 3S + open UDP 8888 — not yet live-tested with real headset

continuity_snapshot.human_purpose: Run physics experiments from the browser; primary focus exp1 angular momentum + stable cloud/Isaac workflow.
continuity_snapshot.browser_remote_server: Use http://<public-ip>:5173 or ssh -L 5173:127.0.0.1:5173; open firewall for 5173/8080/30000 if needed.
continuity_snapshot.isaac_note: Isaac Sim opens as the desktop Kit app; start_server.py runs inside Script Editor, not plain conda python on SSH.
